body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: agent notification system
body.description:
**What changed:** Shim now filters all incoming messages through a notification level (SILENT/QUIET/LOUD) with per-sender overrides and state-linked defaults.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- HALT/priority messages could be silently downgraded if notification level config is misconfigured (HALT must bypass the filter — verify it does)
- QUIET pending list (notify_pending.txt) could grow unbounded if never surfaced — need a cap or auto-clear on context-load
- Devices without tmux sessions (headless, Granny herself) silently fall back to QUIET — could mask LOUD intent
- Config file not-found on first boot → default generation must be idempotent and not noisy
- BaseShim change could break existing shim subclasses that override __init__ without calling super()

**Signals to watch:** Logs show `notif: <sender> → <LEVEL>` on every inbound message. No HALT messages silently queued. notify_pending.txt doesn't grow unbounded. CC gets woken by Granny dispatch when idle.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-21.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
